steps.title: Set the graph height to zero
steps.content:
An $x$-intercept occurs where $$y=0$$ For the graph of $$y=f(x)+5$$, solve:
$$f(x)+5=0$$
Using $$f(x)=4d(24x+25)+15$$, this becomes:
$$4d(24x+25)+20=0$$

steps.title: Solve for the coordinate
steps.content: Expand and isolate $x$. Since $d$ is nonzero, division by $96d$ is allowed. $$96d x+100d+20=0$$ $$96d x=-100d-20$$ $$x=\frac{-100d-20}{96d}$$ So the intercept is $$\big(\frac{-100d-20}{96d},0\big)$$ The answer is B.
